reasoning: The most recent value of the 30-year yield is 4.87% (as of Jan 30, 2026). Over the last month, the yield has fluctuated between 4.79% and 4.91%. Because 4.87% is near the top of this short-term range, mean reversion suggests a decrease is more likely than an increase over the next week. The FRED tool's statistical projection estimates a 35-37% probability of the yield being higher by Feb 8, accounting for trend and historical volatility. While recent news highlights upward pressure from trade war fears and potential supply constraints, occasional weaker economic data (e.g., jobs) balances this out. I anchor to 0.37 for the near-term Feb-8 horizon. For the later Mar-3 horizon, uncertainty increases, so the probability reverts closer to the uninformative baseline of 0.50 (estimated at 0.45).
